name: European TTF Natural Gas
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **TTF EASED TO €46.93/MWh (-2.5% MAY 5) FROM MAY 4'S €48.98 — CEASEFIRE-RELIEF UNWIND MATCHING BRENT.** Trump's May 5 pause of Project Freedom triggered European energy-security tail-risk relief; Deutsche Welle emphasized the energy-implications angle. TTF remains BELOW €70+ cascade-intensity threshold (5-sessions-sustained trigger NOT activated at any point in the May 4-5 stress event). Structural offsets intact: (a) Hormuz still functionally closed Day 68 but kinetic-war probability compressed; (b) European inventory refill window tightening into summer (storage at 5-year seasonal low); (c) Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. ECB has not characterized Hormuz risk as systemic. The May 4-5 stress did NOT push TTF to crisis-cascade levels — paper TTF continues to behave more like inflation event than crisis event, validating the structural-offsets framework. The TTF-Brent decoupling persists: TTF -2.5% May 5 vs Brent -4% — TTF moved less in both directions, confirming structural-anchored pricing. Pre-crisis baseline €31.20; current premium ~50% above baseline.
